rag_rules: Fix type errors by resolving invalid identifiers or removing invalid clauses; do not introduce new architecture elements.
Ensure AGREE expressions use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers; avoid implementation-level references in type scopes.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must preserve type consistency and use only declared or visible identifiers.
Component type annexes should not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths or ports.
Generated AGREE code must align with AADL scope and type rules; avoid package-qualified names not in the model.
When AGREE diagnostics report type mismatches, align helper declarations with their expressions' types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must be typed consistently: bool for Boolean logic, int for integers, real for floating point.
Guarantees and assumptions in component type annexes must reference only visible ports and features.
Helper functions and constants in AGREE must be declared within the same annex and match the model's visible scope.
Avoid using event ports in numeric or Boolean comparisons unless explicitly supported by the model.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by adding new architecture elements; instead, remove or rewrite invalid clauses.
Do not mix implementation-level references with component type contracts; move bindings to implementation annexes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
